headlines.headline: Israel raises flag on Beaufort Castle, expands Lebanon invasion across Litani River
headlines.summary: Israeli forces captured the strategic Beaufort Castle in southern Lebanon and issued evacuation orders for areas south of the Zahrani River, about 40km from the border. The IDF says it has crossed the Litani River as part of expanded operations against Hezbollah.

headlines.headline: Ukrainian drones strike key Russian Saratov oil refinery, pipeline facilities
headlines.summary: Ukraines military confirmed strikes on the Saratov oil refinery (7M tons/year capacity), the Lazarevo pipeline station in Kirov Oblast 1,200km from Ukraine, and a fuel depot in Rostov Oblast. Russia reported intercepting 216 drones across 10 regions.

headlines.headline: Germany delivers new IRIS-T air defense system to Ukraine amid Russian attack surge
headlines.summary: President Zelensky announced Ukraine received a new IRIS-T system from Germany as Russian forces launched over 2,300 drones, 1,560 guided bombs, and 108 missiles in the past week. Ukraine faces critical shortages of air defense missiles.

headlines.headline: Lukashenko warns Belarus has major target in Ukraine amid escalating tensions
headlines.summary: Belarusian leader Alexander Lukashenko said Minsk has one major target with precise coordinates near Belarus, dismissing Ukrainian troops as cannon fodder. The remarks follow joint Belarusian-Russian nuclear drills and Kyivs identification of 500 potential targets in Belarus.

headlines.headline: Trump claims Iran agreed not to develop nuclear weapons, sends revised deal proposal
headlines.summary: President Trump said Iran has guaranteed it wont develop nuclear weapons, while reports indicate Washington sent Tehran a revised peace proposal with tougher terms. Iran maintains it requires release of $12 billion in frozen assets before substantive talks.

headlines.headline: US Blasts European NATO Allies Over Defense Spending
headlines.summary: The United States criticized NATO allies at a Singapore summit over inadequate defense contributions, while European officials insisted the alliance remains resilient amid growing transatlantic tensions.

perspectives.angle: Czech Republic to Miss NATO Defense Spending Target
perspectives.summary: Czech Prime Minister Andrej Babis said the country will "probably" miss NATO's 2% GDP defense spending target this year, highlighting ongoing challenges for European allies in meeting military commitments amid US pressure.

headlines.headline: Iran Partially Restores Internet After Three-Month Shutdown
headlines.summary: Iranian authorities have reinstated limited internet access three months after cutting off the country at the start of the war with the US and Israel, though significant restrictions remain in place.

headlines.headline: Trump Ukraine Aid Cuts Undermine Russian War Crimes Investigations
headlines.summary: US aid reductions are hampering efforts to document and prosecute Russian war crimes in Ukraine, as investigators face challenges gathering evidence amid ongoing conflict.

headlines.headline: IRGC attacks separatist groups in northern Iraq amid regional tensions
headlines.summary: Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps ground forces struck bases of separatist groups in northern Iraq. The operation comes as Iran faces pressure from US negotiations and regional conflicts.

headlines.headline: Myanmar explosion kills over 45 at mining explosives storage facility near China border
headlines.summary: A deadly blast at a building storing explosives for mining in northeastern Myanmar killed more than 45 people and injured dozens. The explosion occurred in Namhkam township near the China border.

headlines.headline: Israeli forces capture strategic Beaufort Castle in deepest Lebanon incursion in 25 years
headlines.summary: Israeli troops seized the Crusader-era castle overlooking southern Lebanon, marking a significant escalation despite a ceasefire agreement. Forces now occupy nearly one-fifth of Lebanese territory.

perspectives.angle: Israel captures Beaufort Castle and pushes past Litani River in deepest Lebanon incursion in 25 years
perspectives.summary: Israeli forces seized the strategic Crusader-era Beaufort Castle and crossed the Litani River, with Netanyahu vowing to expand the offensive. The move marks a dramatic shift in Israel's Lebanon campaign and could reshape the security landscape.

perspectives.angle: Israeli troops capture strategic Beaufort Castle in southern Lebanon
perspectives.summary: Israeli forces seized the 900-year-old Beaufort Castle and its strategic ridge in southern Lebanon, marking a major advance against Hezbollah despite a ceasefire announced over six weeks ago.

headlines.headline: Japan rejects Chinese accusations of new militarism, cites Beijings rapid arms buildup
headlines.summary: Tokyo pushed back against Chinese criticism of its defense policies, accusing China of being the true driver of military expansion in Asia-Pacific.

perspectives.angle: Japan Refutes New Militarism Accusations, Criticizes China Military Buildup
perspectives.summary: Defense Minister Shinjiro Koizumi pushed back against claims of Japanese militarism while criticizing China for rapidly expanding military capabilities without transparency, underscoring deepening tensions between the two nations.

headlines.headline: China patrols Scarborough Shoal after Philippines warns of maritime threat
headlines.summary: Beijing deployed coast guard vessels to the disputed shoal in the South China Sea, escalating tensions with Manila over territorial claims.

perspectives.angle: China conducts combat patrols near Scarborough Shoal amid South China Sea tensions
perspectives.summary: China's military and coast guard carried out patrols near disputed Scarborough Shoal on Sunday, a day after the Philippines warned it remains under "severe threat" from Beijing despite recent US-China tensions easing.

perspectives.angle: Philippines Warns of Ongoing China Threat Despite Trump-Xi Summit
perspectives.summary: Defense Secretary Gilberto Teodoro Jr. stated the Philippines remains under “severe threat” from China despite recent US-China diplomatic thaw, highlighting ongoing regional tensions.

headlines.headline: Ukrainian drones strike pipeline, refinery and fuel depot across multiple Russian regions
headlines.summary: Overnight drone attacks targeted energy infrastructure in several Russian regions, demonstrating Ukraines continued ability to project force deep into Russian territory.

perspectives.angle: Ukrainian drones strike targets across multiple Russian regions
perspectives.summary: Ukrainian drones hit energy and industrial targets across several Russian regions overnight, in an escalating campaign of strikes against infrastructure.

headlines.headline: Trump sends tougher terms to Iran for peace framework as negotiations continue
headlines.summary: The US president revised the proposed deal text with stricter conditions, aiming to pressure Iran into accepting the current framework to end the war.

perspectives.angle: Trump tightens Iran deal terms, says no rush for agreement as war enters day 93
perspectives.summary: President Trump told Fox News he is in "no hurry" for a deal with Iran while US media reported he asked for amendments to toughen terms of the preliminary agreement. Iran accused the US of betraying diplomacy by continuing its naval blockade.

headlines.headline: Colombia Votes in High-Stakes Presidential Election
headlines.summary: Colombians cast ballots in a presidential election choosing between a leftist reformer, a security-focused businessman, and a right-wing senator. The outcome could reshape the country's approach to violence, inequality, and relations with the US.

perspectives.angle: Colombia Votes in Pivotal Presidential Election
perspectives.summary: Colombians head to the polls in a high-stakes presidential election pitting outgoing President Gustavo Petro's leftist allies against right-wing and pro-Trump candidates, a decade after the historic FARC peace deal.

headlines.headline: UK Foreign Minister to Visit China and India for Strategic Talks
headlines.summary: British Foreign Secretary Yvette Cooper will travel to China on Monday and then to India, focusing on the Strait of Hormuz crisis, the Russia-Ukraine war, and the Ebola outbreak. The diplomatic mission comes as the UK seeks to maintain relations with key global powers amid shifting geopolitical dynamics.

perspectives.angle: UK Foreign Minister to Visit China and India Amid Global Crises
perspectives.summary: British Foreign Minister Yvette Cooper will travel to China on Monday and then to India later in the week. The visits will focus on global issues including the Strait of Hormuz, the Russia-Ukraine war, and the Ebola outbreak.

headlines.headline: Brazil Investigates Suspected Ebola Case in Sao Paulo
headlines.summary: Health authorities in Brazil's Sao Paulo state are investigating a suspected Ebola case, raising concerns about potential spread of the outbreak from Africa to South America. The region remains on alert as the outbreak in Congo and Uganda continues.

headlines.headline: Pentagon Accelerates Push for Battlefield AI Amid Caution Calls
headlines.summary: The Trump administration is pushing to integrate artificial intelligence into military operations, despite concerns from defense companies and military leaders about risks. The debate highlights tensions between rapid innovation and battlefield safety.

headlines.headline: Germany Sends New IRIS-T System to Ukraine as Russian Attacks Intensify
headlines.summary: Germany dispatched a new IRIS-T air defense system as Ukraine faces escalating Russian drone and missile attacks, with over 2,300 attack drones and 108 missiles launched in the past week alone.

perspectives.angle: Ukraine receives new IRIS-T air defense system from Germany amid Russian drone threat
perspectives.summary: Ukrainian President Zelensky announced the arrival of a new IRIS-T launcher as Kyiv braces for Russian mass attacks. Germany continues air defense support amid intensified Russian drone and missile strikes.

perspectives.angle: Russian Region Creates Drone Defense Ministry as Ukrainian Strikes Reshape War
perspectives.summary: Nizhny Novgorod Oblast established a new ministry dedicated to defending against Ukrainian drone strikes, highlighting how Russian regions are adapting to the growing threat of Ukrainian attacks deep inside Russia.

perspectives.angle: Hegseth promises US will find way to help Ukraine amid air defense shortage
perspectives.summary: US Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth told reporters the US will "find a way" to help Ukraine defend itself after Zelensky sent an urgent letter to the White House about worsening air defense shortages.

headlines.headline: China Building Launch Pads Near Nuclear Missile Silos
headlines.summary: Satellite imagery reveals China is constructing a sprawling network of military sites near its nuclear silos, including launch pads that could enhance the readiness of its nuclear forces capable of reaching US cities.

perspectives.angle: China Building Launch Pads Near Nuclear Missile Silos
perspectives.summary: Satellite imagery reveals China constructing a sprawling network of military sites near its nuclear silos, raising concerns about expanded strategic capabilities.

headlines.headline: Pentagon Accelerates $4B SpaceX Deal for Global Aircraft Tracking From Orbit
headlines.summary: A new $4 billion SpaceX contract aims to build a satellite network capable of tracking aircraft worldwide, potentially making traditional airborne early warning aircraft redundant.

perspectives.angle: Pentagon's $4B SpaceX deal accelerates plans for global aircraft tracking from orbit
perspectives.summary: A new satellite network to track aircraft from space could offer unprecedented global surveillance capability and potentially make airborne early warning aircraft redundant, representing a major shift in military air defense architecture.

headlines.headline: Japan Defence Minister Slams China's "Huge Arsenal" at Singapore Summit
headlines.summary: Shinjiro Koizumi rebuffed Beijing's accusations of "new militarism," arguing China's nuclear arsenal and lack of transparency are the real threat to regional stability. The comments at the Shangri-La Dialogue mark Japan's most pointed response yet to Chinese criticism of its defence build-up.

headlines.headline: US Stops Another Merchant Ship in Iran Port Blockade
headlines.summary: The US military fired a missile into the engine room of a Gambia-flagged cargo ship that ignored 20+ warnings trying to enter an Iranian port. Six ships have now been stopped under the blockade launched in response to Iran's closure of the Strait of Hormuz.

headlines.headline: France Arrests 780 After Champions League Final Riots
headlines.summary: Nearly 800 people were arrested and 219 injured—including 57 police—after PSG's Champions League victory sparked violent clashes across France. One person was found dead near the Paris ring road.

headlines.headline: US Congress Advances Bill to Integrate Israeli Military Industry
headlines.summary: A provision in the 2027 defence authorization act would bind US and Israeli weapons industries closer than ever, creating a single coordinator for joint research, production, and military systems.

headlines.headline: Russian drone hits residential building in Romania, NATO member on edge
headlines.summary: A Russian Geran-2 (Shahed) drone struck an apartment building in the Romanian city of Galati near the Ukrainian border, injuring civilians and heightening concerns about spillover from the Ukraine war. Residents expressed fear about inadequate air defenses.

perspectives.angle: Russian Drone Strikes Romanian Apartment Building Near Ukraine Border
perspectives.summary: A Russian Geran-2 drone crashed into an apartment block in Galati, Romania, injuring two people. The incident marks the most serious NATO territory strike since the Ukraine war began. Romania's president confirmed it was a Russian Shahed drone that veered off course.

headlines.headline: US, UK and Australia announce underwater drone project to protect undersea cables
headlines.summary: Defense ministers from the three AUKUS partners unveiled a new underwater drone technology initiative at the Shangri-La Dialogue in Singapore. The project aims to protect critical undersea infrastructure, with the UK contributing £150m. The technology is expected to be ready by next year.

perspectives.angle: AUKUS Partners Announce Underwater Drone Technology Project
perspectives.summary: The US, UK and Australia unveiled a new uncrewed underwater vehicle project to protect undersea cables and enhance naval defense, with the UK contributing £150 million—the first major deliverable under AUKUS Pillar Two as the alliance seeks to counter Chinese maritime expansion.

headlines.headline: Europe's sustainable jet fuel sector sees opportunity in Iran war disruption
headlines.summary: The Iran war and Strait of Hormuz closure are boosting interest in e-SAF (electro-Sustainable Aviation Fuel) as Europe seeks energy independence. Germany's armed forces tested domestic e-SAF, and governments see strategic value in reducing reliance on Middle Eastern oil.

headlines.headline: Typhoon Jangmi to Bring Heavy Rain to Japan This Week
headlines.summary: Japan Meteorological Agency warns of warning-level rainfall up to 300mm along Pacific coast through June 3. Airlines cancel flights to Okinawa as typhoon approaches southern islands.

headlines.headline: China Factory Activity Stalls as Demand Weakens
headlines.summary: Chinese manufacturing activity flatlined in May as new export orders contracted and input costs rose, official data showed, raising concerns about the world's second-largest economy losing momentum.

perspectives.angle: China Factory Activity Stalls in May as Demand Weakens
perspectives.summary: Chinas manufacturing sector showed signs of strain in May as factory activity flatlined, with new export orders contracting and input costs rising, raising concerns about the worlds second-largest economy.

headlines.headline: US rebukes NATO allies over defense spending at Singapore summit
headlines.summary: The US criticized European NATO members for insufficient defense contributions while the alliance sought to reassure Asian partners. Western European officials insisted the alliance remains resilient despite tensions.

perspectives.angle: US Allies Call for Unity at Singapore Defense Summit
perspectives.summary: American allies emphasized the need for global cooperation to address cross-regional security threats, even as the Trump administration criticizes traditional partners over burden-sharing.

headlines.headline: Rights groups sue over conditions at largest US immigration detention center
headlines.summary: Civil rights groups filed a lawsuit alleging human rights abuses at the El Paso, Texas facility where three people have died in the nine months since it opened.

headlines.headline: New Jersey governor blames out-of-state agitators for Newark ICE detention protests
headlines.summary: Governor Mikie Sherrill said outside agitators escalated tensions at protests outside a Newark immigrant detention center, as the city imposed an overnight curfew around the Delaney Hall facility.

headlines.headline: US Forces Strike Cargo Ship in Gulf of Oman After Blockade Warnings
headlines.summary: US forces attacked the Gambia-flagged cargo ship Lian Star in the Gulf of Oman, claiming it had ignored over 20 warnings before striking its engine room. A senior Iranian official accused Trump of "betraying diplomacy for the third time."

headlines.headline: Australia to Receive Only Used US Nuclear Submarines Under Amended AUKUS Deal
headlines.summary: The US will only send used nuclear-powered submarines to Australia under a "streamlined" AUKUS agreement, described as a cost-effective measure. The announcement came at Singapore's Shangri-La Dialogue.

perspectives.angle: AUKUS Deal Modified: Australia to Receive Used US Submarines
perspectives.summary: Australia, the US and UK agreed to streamline the AUKUS submarine partnership, allowing Australia to receive used US Virginia-class submarines as part of the trilateral defense pact.

headlines.headline: Indo-Pacific nations deepen defense ties amid US reliability doubts
headlines.summary: Caught between China rapid military rise and growing doubts about US focus, Indo-Pacific nations are racing to arm themselves and establish deeper mutual defense partnerships.

headlines.headline: South Korea, Japan discuss military logistics pact amid regional security concerns
headlines.summary: Seoul and Tokyo are exploring a military-logistics support agreement at the Shangri-La Dialogue, as Indo-Pacific nations race to strengthen defense ties amid China's rise and uncertainty about US commitment.

headlines.headline: India signs BrahMos missile deal with Vietnam, final talks with Indonesia underway
headlines.summary: India announced it has signed a deal to supply BrahMos supersonic cruise missiles to Vietnam and is in "final stages" for a similar agreement with Indonesia, expanding its defense exports in Southeast Asia.

headlines.headline: US planning accelerated troop withdrawal from Europe, German newspaper reports
headlines.summary: The US plans to accelerate troop withdrawal from European bases and will present proposals to NATO allies next month, according to a report citing an unidentified Pentagon source.

perspectives.angle: US Planning Accelerated Troop Withdrawal from Europe
perspectives.summary: The Pentagon plans to speed up the withdrawal of US troops from European bases and will present proposals to NATO allies next month, according to German media citing an unidentified Pentagon source.

headlines.headline: Europe's Green Jet Fuels Gain Strategic Value After Iran War Disrupts Oil Routes
headlines.summary: The Iran war's impact on fuel supplies is boosting interest in synthetic aviation fuels (e-SAF), with European militaries eyeing domestic production for energy security. Costs remain 10x higher than kerosene.

headlines.headline: Malta Votes in Snap Election Amid Geopolitical Uncertainty
headlines.summary: Prime Minister Robert Abela called early elections seeking a fresh mandate to navigate Middle East conflict fallout. Tourism-dependent Malta faces fuel cost pressures. Labour expected to win a fourth term.

headlines.headline: Aukus Nations Announce Underwater Drone Project to Protect Sea Cables
headlines.summary: The US, UK and Australia will develop unmanned undersea vehicles under the Aukus defense pact, with the UK contributing £150m. The technology aims to protect undersea cables and boost naval defense capabilities, with deployment expected by 2027.

perspectives.angle: AUKUS Partners Announce Underwater Drone Project to Protect Sea Cables
perspectives.summary: The US, UK and Australia unveiled plans to develop underwater drone technology under their AUKUS military pact, aimed at protecting undersea cables and boosting naval defense capabilities. UK will contribute £150m with the technology expected ready by next year.

headlines.headline: Israeli Soldiers Describe Ongoing Killings in Gaza Despite Ceasefire
headlines.summary: AP interviews with Israeli reservists reveal continued shooting of Palestinians near the 'yellow line' dividing Gaza. Over 900 people have been killed since October's ceasefire, with soldiers describing permissive rules of engagement and orders to 'shoot to kill' anyone crossing the line.

headlines.headline: US plans Coast Guard cutter transfer to Philippines amid South China Sea tensions
headlines.summary: The United States announced plans to transfer a Coast Guard cutter to the Philippines to strengthen Manila's maritime security capabilities in the contested South China Sea.

headlines.headline: Malaysia questions Norway's apology over cancelled missile deal
headlines.summary: Malaysian officials say Norway's apology for unilaterally cancelling the Naval Strike Missile export license has raised more questions, casting doubt on the future of the defense procurement.

headlines.headline: WHO Chief Visits Epicenter of Ebola Outbreak in DR Congo
headlines.summary: The WHO Director-General toured the heart of the Democratic Republic of Congos Ebola outbreak, warning that the spread is outpacing the global response and calling for more international funding.

perspectives.angle: Ebola outbreak in Congo and Uganda reaches 263 confirmed cases with 43 deaths
perspectives.summary: A rare strain of Ebola has spread across the Democratic Republic of Congo and Uganda, with cases outpacing the response. Africa CDC reports 263 confirmed cases as health authorities struggle to contain the outbreak.

headlines.headline: Philippines Defense Chief Warns China Threat Persists Despite US-China Thaw
headlines.summary: Manilas defense minister said the Philippines remains under severe threat from China despite the recent Trump-Xi summit that eased tensions between Washington and Beijing.

headlines.headline: Austrian Protesters Block Key Alpine Motorway Connecting Germany and Italy
headlines.summary: Thousands of local residents shut down the Brenner motorway, a vital north-south corridor through the Alps, in protest against trucks and tourists clogging their roads.

headlines.headline: SoftBank to Invest €45 Billion in AI Data Centers in France
headlines.summary: Japan's SoftBank Group announced plans to invest €45 billion over five years to build artificial intelligence infrastructure in France, marking a major expansion of AI computing capacity in Europe.

headlines.headline: Iran Claims It Shot Down US Military Drone Over Territorial Waters
headlines.summary: Iranian military says it detected and destroyed an MQ-1 Predator UAV after it entered Iranian airspace on a hostile mission, escalating tensions as nuclear deal negotiations continue.

headlines.headline: Euphrates Flooding Displaces Thousands in Eastern Syria
headlines.summary: Syrian authorities opened dam spillways for the first time in three decades as rising Euphrates waters forced thousands to flee in Deir Ezzor province.

headlines.headline: US Military Fires on Blockade Runner Attempting to Reach Iranian Port
headlines.summary: U.S. Central Command disabled a Gambia-flagged vessel with a Hellfire missile after it ignored warnings while attempting to breach the American blockade of Iranian ports, marking an escalation in enforcement of the Strait of Hormuz closure.

perspectives.angle: US Military Stops Sixth Ship Attempting to Break Iran Blockade
perspectives.summary: A Gambian-flagged cargo vessel, the Lian Star, was disabled by a US missile strike after ignoring 20+ warnings while trying to reach an Iranian port. The US has now stopped six ships and redirected 116 since implementing the blockade on April 17.

perspectives.angle: Trump Holds Iran Deal Meeting as US Stops Another Blockade Runner
perspectives.summary: President Trump met with top aides in the White House Situation Room to decide on extending the Iran ceasefire, while US Central Command reported stopping a Gambian-flagged cargo ship attempting to breach the Iranian port blockade—the sixth ship interdicted since April.

headlines.headline: China Absent from Shangri-La Defense Forum as Delegates Ask Where is China
headlines.summary: Chinese Defense Minister Dong Jun skipped the annual Asian security summit again this year, sending only a low-profile delegation of PLA experts and scholars as Beijing maintains its absence from key regional defense dialogues.

headlines.headline: Vietnam Leader Emphasizes Importance of Strong China Ties for Regional Peace
headlines.summary: Vietnam top leader stated that strong relations with China would benefit regional peace and security, while also noting the importance of U.S. ties, as Vietnam navigates its position between the two rival powers.

headlines.headline: Drone Strike Hits Zaporizhzhia Nuclear Plant, Russia and Ukraine Trade Blame
headlines.summary: Russia's state nuclear agency said a Ukrainian drone struck a turbine hall at the Zaporizhzhia nuclear plant, leaving a hole in the wall. Kyiv denied responsibility.

perspectives.angle: Ukraine strikes Russian energy sites, denies hitting Zaporizhzhia nuclear plant
perspectives.summary: Ukrainian drones attacked fuel storage and refinery facilities in Rostov and Saratov regions. Kyiv rejected Russian claims that a Ukrainian drone struck the Kremlin-occupied Zaporizhzhia nuclear plant.

headlines.headline: Pentagon Chief Warns US Ready to Resume Iran Strikes If Peace Deal Fails
headlines.summary: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth said the US military is prepared to restart strikes on Iran if negotiations collapse, speaking at the Shangri-La defense forum in Singapore.

perspectives.angle: US Warns It Can Resume War With Iran as Ceasefire Deal Stalls
perspectives.summary: The United States warned it is 'more than capable' of resuming military operations against Iran if a ceasefire agreement cannot be reached, amid ongoing tensions over shipping in the Strait of Hormuz.

headlines.headline: Iran Accuses Trump of Stalling Talks With Excessive Demands
headlines.summary: An Iranian official said Trump is making excessive demands that stall peace negotiations, as oil prices dip on reports a deal could be close but remains uncertain.

headlines.headline: SoftBank Plans $88 Billion AI Data Hub in France
headlines.summary: SoftBank founder Masayoshi Son announced the massive investment after meeting President Macron, with France's status as an energy exporter playing a key role in the decision.

headlines.headline: China Building Launch Pads Near Nuclear Missile Silos
headlines.summary: Satellite images reveal Beijing is building a web of military sites near its nuclear silos, with launch pads that could enhance China's strategic capabilities as its missiles can reach any US city.

headlines.headline: US General Holds Rare Meeting With Cuban Military Officials Near Guantanamo
headlines.summary: US Southern Command commander met with Cuban military officials in the first such high-level meeting in recent memory, amid Cuban concerns about possible US military action.

headlines.headline: Trump roils waters on Taiwan independence after Beijing summit
headlines.summary: Debate over US president's China trip reveals varied interpretations of his stance on Taiwan, with Trump saying the US is "not looking to have somebody say, let's go independent."

headlines.headline: US general holds rare meeting with Cuban military officials near Guantanamo
headlines.summary: The first meeting in recent memory between a US Southern Command head and Cuban military officials comes amid growing concerns in Cuba about possible US military action.

headlines.headline: Ousted Turkish opposition leader Özel draws thousands in Ankara protest
headlines.summary: Özgür Özel, removed from office by court ruling, demonstrated strong support with a major rally in Ankara, challenging President Erdoğan's grip on power ahead of elections.

headlines.headline: China expands digital yuan with lottery draws and fiscal spending trials
headlines.summary: Beijing is accelerating the rollout of its central bank digital currency through expanded pilot programs, positioning the digital yuan as a potential rival to the US dollar in global finance.

headlines.headline: Zambia clears suspected Ebola cases, steps up border screening
headlines.summary: Zambia investigated and cleared two suspected Ebola cases while intensifying surveillance at its border with the Democratic Republic of Congo amid regional health concerns.

headlines.headline: Turkey sees strong potential for drone development cooperation with Japan
headlines.summary: Turkish Foreign Minister Hakan Fidan tells Nikkei Asia that Turkey is eager to cooperate with Japan on unmanned aerial systems, including joint development and co-production of drones.

perspectives.angle: Turkey sees strong potential for drone development cooperation with Japan
perspectives.summary: Turkish Foreign Minister Hakan Fidan discussed expanding defense cooperation with Japan in an interview with Nikkei Asia, also noting that a US-Iran deal appeared "closer than ever" amid ongoing nuclear negotiations.

headlines.headline: Russia Recalls Ambassador to Armenia Over EU Rapprochement
headlines.summary: Russia recalled its ambassador to Armenia for consultations in protest at Yerevan's deepening ties with the European Union ahead of a crucial June 7 election, signaling mounting tensions in the South Caucasus.

perspectives.angle: Russia Recalls Ambassador to Armenia Over EU Rapprochement
perspectives.summary: Moscow recalled its envoy to Yerevan for consultations in protest of Armenia's deepening ties with the European Union ahead of a June 7 election, signaling growing tensions in Russia's traditional sphere of influence.

headlines.headline: Philippines Defense Minister Warns China Threat Persists Despite Trump-Xi Summit
headlines.summary: The Philippines remains under 'severe threat' from China, Defense Minister said Saturday, despite recent U.S.-China tensions easing after the Trump-Xi summit earlier this month.

headlines.headline: Myanmar Junta Chief Turned President Makes First Foreign Visit to India
headlines.summary: Min Aung Hlaing, who transitioned from junta chief to president less than two months ago, will visit India on Saturday in his first overseas trip, with analysts noting his focus on balancing China's influence.

perspectives.angle: Myanmar Junta Chief Makes First Foreign Visit to India
perspectives.summary: Min Aung Hlaing, who recently transitioned from junta chief to president, is visiting India in his first overseas trip since taking the civilian role, seeking regional legitimacy while continuing a brutal civil war.

headlines.headline: Pentagon Chief Sounds Alarm Over China Buildup, Urges Allies to Boost Defense Spending
headlines.summary: U.S. Defense Secretary urged allies to increase defense spending at a major Asian defense forum, warning that China's growing military dominance requires a coordinated response from regional partners.

headlines.headline: China-Japan rare earth trade fears grow amid diplomatic silence
headlines.summary: With bilateral relations at a low point and Beijing enacting limited export controls on critical minerals, Japanese industries fear shipments of rare earth elements could be delayed or halted.

headlines.headline: Chinese Scientists Create AI Algorithm for Autonomous Drone Swarms
headlines.summary: New Smart Web system enables drone swarms to identify friend, foe, and terrain independently, allowing coordinated attacks even when communications are jammed.

perspectives.angle: Chinese scientists develop algorithm for autonomous drone swarm warfare
perspectives.summary: Chinese researchers have published a paper describing an algorithm capable of achieving 100% kill rates in drone warfare by autonomously identifying targets as friend, foe, or terrain even when communications are jammed.

headlines.headline: Pentagon chief urges allies to boost defense spending over China alarm
headlines.summary: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth urged allies to increase defense spending to counter China's growing dominance in the region during the Shangri-La Dialogue in Singapore.

perspectives.angle: US Defense Chief Hegseth Reassures Asia Allies, Calls for Defense Spending
perspectives.summary: US Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth told the Shangri-La Dialogue that America is not turning its back on Asia despite the Iran war, calling on regional allies to spend 3.5% of GDP on defense while avoiding needless confrontation with China.

headlines.headline: India warns inflation may rise from Middle East conflict and weak monsoon
headlines.summary: India's finance ministry warned that retail inflation could accelerate due to fuel price hikes and weaker-than-normal monsoon rains as energy supply disruptions continue from the Middle East conflict.

headlines.headline: Delegates ask "Where is China?" at Asian defense forum
headlines.summary: Chinese Defense Minister Dong Jun skipped the Shangri-La Dialogue again this year. Beijing sent a low-profile delegation of PLA "experts and scholars" instead.

headlines.headline: Russia Destroys Sumy Train Station, Kills 5 in Ukraine-Wide Strikes
headlines.summary: Russian forces destroyed a civilian train station in Shostka, Sumy Oblast with kamikaze drones and launched 290 drones plus missiles across Ukraine, killing 5 and injuring 40. The station served hundreds of residents daily before being targeted.

perspectives.angle: Ukraine Strikes Oil Infrastructure and Tanker in Western Russia and Occupied Crimea
perspectives.summary: Ukrainian forces carried out coordinated strikes on oil infrastructure across Russian and Russian-held territories overnight May 30, according to Russian Telegram channels. The attacks targeted fuel storage and transport facilities.

headlines.headline: Japan PM Vows Breakthrough on North Korea Abduction Issue
headlines.summary: Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi pledged to resolve the decades-old abduction issue with North Korea, including considering summit talks. Japan lists 17 citizens abducted in the 1970s-80s, with only 5 returned in 2002.

headlines.headline: Record-Breaking Heatwave Hits Europe, Multiple Deaths Reported
headlines.summary: France, Britain and Portugal all recorded their hottest May days ever, with temperatures exceeding 37°C in parts of France. Several heat-related deaths reported across northern Europe as storms approach to break the heat.

headlines.headline: Hegseth Addresses Shangri-La Dialogue on US Foreign Policy
headlines.summary: US Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth outlined Americas geopolitical goals at the Shangri-La Dialogue in Singapore, addressing defense leaders amid questions about US commitment to Asian allies and ongoing global conflicts.

headlines.headline: Macron Faces Backlash After Interrupting Africa Summit Panel
headlines.summary: French President Emmanuel Macron faced criticism after he stormed the stage at the Africa Forward Summit in Kenya to demand silence from the audience. The incident comes as France attempts to reshape its relationship with African nations as a partnership of equals rather than a former colonial power.

headlines.headline: China dials down rhetoric but regional risks persist at Shangri-La Dialogue
headlines.summary: Chinese delegation avoids sharp criticism of US following Xi-Trump summit, but warns of challenges from "hegemonism" and "bloc confrontation" without directly naming Washington.

headlines.headline: Israel Added to UN Sexual Violence Blacklist for First Time
headlines.summary: The United Nations has added Israel to its blacklist of parties responsible for sexual violence in conflict zones, marking an unprecedented diplomatic blow for the country amid ongoing international scrutiny of its military operations.

perspectives.angle: Israel Added to UN Sexual Violence in Conflict Blacklist for First Time
perspectives.summary: The UN Secretary-General added Israel to the list of parties credibly suspected of sexual violence in conflict zones, drawing Israeli rejection and diplomatic tensions.

headlines.headline: Iran Accuses Trump of Betraying Diplomacy as Hormuz Blockade Continues
headlines.summary: An adviser to Iran Supreme Leader warned that Trump is betraying diplomacy for the third time by maintaining the naval blockade in the Strait of Hormuz, demanding authorization for all commercial vessels transiting the strategic waterway.

headlines.headline: Russia Recalls Ambassador to Armenia Over EU Pivot
headlines.summary: Russia recalled its ambassador from Armenia a day after Putin warned Yerevan against seeking EU membership, referencing the Ukrainian scenario. Armenia has been pivoting away from Russia toward the West amid regional tensions.

headlines.headline: India Signs BrahMos Missile Deal with Vietnam, Nears Pact with Indonesia
headlines.summary: India signed a defense agreement with Vietnam for BrahMos missiles and is close to finalizing a similar deal with Indonesia, as New Delhi deepens military partnerships with ASEAN nations amid regional tensions.

headlines.headline: Nigeria Puts 21 States on Ebola Alert as Regional Outbreak Spreads
headlines.summary: Nigerian health authorities placed 21 states on alert for Ebola virus importation as neighboring DRC battles a growing outbreak. The WHO confirmed cases have nearly doubled in days, with regional spread concerns mounting.

headlines.headline: US Defense Secretary Hegseth Tones Down China Rhetoric at Singapore Forum
headlines.summary: At the Shangri-La defense forum, Pentagon chief Pete Hegseth assured Pacific allies of US commitment to the region while softening previous warnings about China, following Trump’s recent Beijing summit with Xi Jinping.

headlines.headline: IMF, World Bank, IEA Chiefs Warn of Summer Fuel Scarcity from Hormuz Crisis
headlines.summary: Heads of major international economic institutions warned that prolonged closure of the Strait of Hormuz could cause severe fuel shortages during peak summer demand, straining global energy supplies.

headlines.headline: US General holds rare meeting with Cuban military officials near Guantanamo Bay
headlines.summary: The head of US Southern Command met with Cuban military officials for the first time in recent memory amid growing concerns in Cuba about possible US military action.

headlines.headline: China creates autonomous drone warfare algorithm for jamming environments
headlines.summary: Chinese scientists developed a smart web algorithm that identifies objects as friend, foe or terrain, enabling drone swarms to operate when communication is jammed and vision is blocked.

headlines.headline: 17 nations launch pact to protect undersea cables without US and China
headlines.summary: Singapore announced a new pact with 17 nations to collaborate on protecting vital undersea cables, with both the US and China notably absent from the agreement.

headlines.headline: US softens tone on China at Shangri-La Dialogue after Xi-Trump summit
headlines.summary: Defense Secretary Hegseth avoided directly describing Beijing as a threat at the regional security forum, in contrast to previous years, following the recent Trump-Xi summit.

headlines.headline: Lula Slams US Designation of Brazilian Gangs as Terrorist Organizations
headlines.summary: Brazil's President Lula da Silva said Brazil will not be treated as a 'tinpot country' after the US designated the PCC and Red Command cartels as foreign terrorist organizations. The move is seen as a boost for Lula's far-right challenger Flavio Bolsonaro in October's presidential election.

headlines.headline: EU Set to Release 16 Billion Euros to Hungary After Orban's Defeat
headlines.summary: The European Union is preparing to release 16 billion euros in previously withheld funds to Hungary after the defeat of Viktor Orban's government. The funds were frozen due to rule of law and anti-corruption concerns, and will now be released as Hungary enacts required reforms.

headlines.headline: Mexico Senate Passes Bill Allowing Election Annulment for Foreign Interference
headlines.summary: Mexico's senate approved a constitutional amendment that would allow election results to be annulled for 'foreign interference,' defined to include illicit financing, propaganda, misinformation and digital manipulation. Critics say the bill could give the ruling party power to overturn opposition victories.

headlines.headline: Iranian Hard-Liners Mobilize to Derail Potential US Peace Deal
headlines.summary: A political fight is unfolding in Iran as hard-liners use rallies, state media and public statements to undermine negotiations with the US. The faction opposes concessions to Washington as Trump deliberates on a framework agreement to end the conflict.

headlines.headline: Italy cancels Kanye West and Travis Scott concerts over security concerns
headlines.summary: Italian authorities banned two concerts in Reggio Emilia due to public order concerns and potential protests. West has faced cancellations across Europe following antisemitic remarks, while Scott has been under scrutiny since the Astroworld crowd crush that killed 10 people.

headlines.headline: Truck crash kills 22 Afghan refugees returning from Pakistan
headlines.summary: A truck carrying Afghan refugees returning from Pakistan overturned in eastern Afghanistan, killing at least 22 people including 10 children. The crash comes as millions of Afghans have returned from Pakistan and Iran following crackdowns on migrants.

headlines.headline: Spain legalizes over 500,000 undocumented immigrants in regularization program
headlines.summary: Spain is processing over 549,000 applications to regularize undocumented immigrants by June 30, a stark contrast to US deportation policies. Prime Minister Sánchez called it an act of normalization for people already part of Spanish daily life.

headlines.headline: Hegseth tells Asian allies to boost defence spending for US support
headlines.summary: US Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth said Asian partners must do more to get more from Washington, telling the Shangri-La Dialogue that countries wanting US assistance should increase their own defense spending.

headlines.headline: Germany deploys troops to Lithuania in historic NATO defense move
headlines.summary: Berlin has deployed forces to protect the Baltic country amid fears of increased Russian aggression in Europe. German troops returned to Lithuania 80 years after Nazi occupation, this time welcomed as defenders.

perspectives.angle: German Troops Return to Lithuania as NATO Shield Against Russia
perspectives.summary: Germany has deployed forces to protect Lithuania amid fears of increased Russian aggression, marking a historic return of German troops to a country they once occupied. This time, they were welcomed.

headlines.headline: Japan purchases $14 million in military equipment for Ukraine under NATO program
headlines.summary: Japan contributed non-lethal aid to Ukraine through the Prioritized Ukraine Requirements List initiative, marking a significant expansion of Tokyo's support for Kyiv amid the ongoing conflict with Russia.

perspectives.angle: Japan buys $14 million in military equipment for Ukraine under NATO program
perspectives.summary: Japan contributes non-lethal military aid to Ukraine through the NATO Prioritized Ukraine Requirements List initiative, expanding Asian support for Kyiv.

headlines.headline: Ghana Passes Anti-LGBTQ+ Bill Criminalizing Same-Sex Relationships
headlines.summary: Ghana parliament approved legislation imposing jail terms for same-sex acts and for identifying as LGBTQ+, joining other African nations with restrictive laws amid Western criticism.

perspectives.angle: Ghana Parliament Passes Bill Criminalizing LGBTQ+ Identity
perspectives.summary: Ghana parliament approved legislation punishing same-sex acts with up to 3 years in prison. Bill introduces duty to report prohibited acts to police, awaiting presidential ratification.

headlines.headline: Kenyan Court Blocks US Plan for Ebola Quarantine Facility
headlines.summary: A Kenyan court suspended construction of a US military-built facility designed to isolate Americans exposed to Ebola, amid local opposition and concerns about hosting foreign disease containment operations.

headlines.headline: Zelensky warns Russia preparing new large-scale attack on Ukraine
headlines.summary: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky stated on May 29 that Ukrainian intelligence has information indicating Russia is preparing a new large-scale attack on Ukraine, amid ongoing ceasefire negotiations in the Iran-US conflict.

headlines.headline: Blue Origin New Glenn rocket explodes during test firing at Cape Canaveral
headlines.summary: Jeff Bezos' Blue Origin suffered a major setback when its New Glenn rocket exploded during a static test fire, destroying part of the launch pad. The explosion creates uncertainty for NASA's Artemis moon program, which relies on Blue Origin landers for upcoming lunar missions.

perspectives.angle: Blue Origin Rocket Explodes During Test, Casting Doubt on NASA Moon Plans
perspectives.summary: A Blue Origin New Glenn rocket exploded on the launch pad at Cape Canaveral during testing, creating a major setback for both the company and NASA Artemis Moon program which relies on Blue Origin vehicles.

headlines.headline: Rwanda signs nuclear cooperation agreement with Russia amid shifting Africa alliances
headlines.summary: Rwanda signed a memorandum of understanding with Russia covering nuclear medicine, research, and potential small modular reactors. The deal reflects Moscow's growing influence in Africa as Western powers face questions about reliability as partners.

perspectives.angle: Rwanda Signs Nuclear Deal With Russia as Africa Shifts Power Balance
perspectives.summary: Rwanda has turned to Russia for nuclear training and research, advancing its ambitions in technology, healthcare and diplomacy while underscoring Africa changing geopolitical alignments.

headlines.headline: Chinese scientists create autonomous drone swarm algorithm for combat
headlines.summary: New AI algorithm enables drone swarms to identify and engage targets independently even when communications are jammed, advancing Chinas military drone capabilities.

headlines.headline: China building launch pads near nuclear missile silos
headlines.summary: Satellite imagery reveals Beijing constructing military infrastructure near its nuclear silo fields, raising concerns about Chinas nuclear force expansion.

headlines.headline: Poland threatens to strip Zelenskyy of top state honour over WWII unit naming
headlines.summary: Poland's President Nawrocki proposed revoking Zelenskyy's Order of the White Eagle after Ukraine named a military unit after the UPA, which was involved in the Volhynia massacres that killed 100,000 Poles. The move strains Poland-Ukraine relations.

headlines.headline: Hegseth Reassures Asia Allies on US Commitment Despite Iran War
headlines.summary: US Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth told the Shangri-La Dialogue that the US is not turning its back on Asia while fulfilling global obligations, calling for allies to spend 3.5% of GDP on defense while avoiding needless confrontation with China.

headlines.headline: Canada Strengthens Chinese Trade Ties Amid US Trade Rifts
headlines.summary: Canada is seeking economic diversification and strengthening trade ties with China amid growing trade tensions with the United States, as Alberta separatists gain momentum in what could be Canadas biggest unity crisis in decades.

headlines.headline: Hegseth Calls for Stable Equilibrium Against China Hegemony at Shangri-La
headlines.summary: The US Defense Secretary emphasized Taiwan arms sales remain decoupled from Iran war decisions, outlining America strategic posture in the Indo-Pacific.

headlines.headline: Vietnam Leader To Lam Stresses Balance Amid Rising Pressure at Shangri-La
headlines.summary: Vietnam emphasized its balanced diplomatic approach at the Shangri-La Dialogue as regional tensions increase between major powers.

headlines.headline: Australian MQ-28 Ghost Bat Drone Now Flying Over Pacific From US Navy Base
headlines.summary: Boeing tests of the Australian-born MQ-28 off California demonstrate maturity and promote sales potential to Pentagon and allies.

headlines.headline: Ukrainian Gripen Fighters to Arrive in 2027 With Long-Range Meteor Missiles
headlines.summary: Sweden transfer of Gripens will give Ukraine dispersed operations capability paired with Meteor missiles and radar integration.

headlines.headline: Japan Immigration Crackdown Hits Foreign Curry Shop Owners
headlines.summary: Under Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi, new visa rules are forcing some foreign business owners who have put down roots in Japan to leave, affecting Sri Lankan and other immigrant communities.

headlines.headline: Trump Boat Strikes Fail to Curb Cocaine Flow to US, Experts Say
headlines.summary: Despite rising body counts off the South American coast from Trump boat strikes policy, researchers say cocaine remains as easily available in many parts of the United States as before the strikes began.

headlines.headline: China building launch pads near nuclear missile silos
headlines.summary: Satellite images reveal China is building a sprawling web of military sites near its nuclear silos, with launch pads that could enhance the survivability and rapid deployment of its nuclear forces.

headlines.headline: Putin Preparing Major Escalation Against Kyiv as Russian Forces Stalled in Ukraine
headlines.summary: Russia warns of systematic missile strikes on Kyiv as battlefield gains stall. Putin seeks to shift domestic war narrative with aerial attacks while Ukraine expands successful long-range drone strikes on Russian energy facilities.

perspectives.angle: Putin Signals Escalation as Russian Forces Stall in Ukraine
perspectives.summary: With battlefield advances grinding to a halt and war fatigue growing at home, Putin appears ready to escalate aerial attacks on Kyiv. Russian losses have nearly tripled compared to last year, and Ukraine has retaken territory around Dnipropetrovsk.

headlines.headline: WhatsApp Messages Undermine Netanyahu Mossad Chief Pick
headlines.summary: Private WhatsApp correspondence reportedly undermines the alibi of Netanyahu controversial pick for Mossad chief, raising questions about the appointment process.

headlines.headline: China Building Launch Pads Near Nuclear Missile Silos
headlines.summary: Satellite imagery reveals China is constructing launch pads near its nuclear missile silos, raising concerns about expansion of nuclear capabilities.

perspectives.angle: China Building Launch Pads Near Its Nuclear Missile Silos
perspectives.summary: China nuclear missiles can reach any U.S. city. New satellite images show Beijing is building a sprawling web of military sites near its nuclear silos.

headlines.headline: Trump ICE Blitz Cost 668,000 Jobs Creating Chilling Effect
headlines.summary: A new report estimates Trump immigration crackdown in US cities destroyed 668,000 jobs, causing labor shortages and forcing companies to reduce operations.

headlines.headline: Ex-California Mayor Pleads Guilty to Acting as China Agent
headlines.summary: Former Arcadia, California mayor Eileen Wang pleaded guilty to acting as an illegal agent for the Chinese government, faces up to 10 years in prison.

headlines.headline: Russian Drone Hits Romanian Apartment Building, Injuring Two
headlines.summary: A Russian drone targeting Ukraine struck a residential building in Romania, injuring two people. Romania says the drone was likely hit over Ukraine by its air defenses.

perspectives.angle: Russian Drone Strikes Romanian Apartment Building, Injures Two
perspectives.summary: A Russian Shahed drone hit a residential building in Galati, Romania near the Ukrainian border, injuring a woman and her teenage son. Romania confirmed it was a Russian Geran-2 drone and closed a Russian consulate in response, calling the incident reckless.

perspectives.angle: Russian Drone Strikes Residential Building in NATO Member Romania
perspectives.summary: A Russian drone hit an apartment block in eastern Romania, causing a fire and injuring two people. The incident marks a significant escalation as Russia directly affects NATO territory amid ongoing Ukraine conflict.

headlines.headline: Putin Ready to Escalate Ukraine War Amid Stalemate and Domestic Discontent
headlines.summary: With a stalemate on the battlefield and growing discontent at home, Putin appears ready to escalate the war in Ukraine, according to AP analysis.

headlines.headline: Spain PM Fights for Political Survival Amid Wave of Scandals
headlines.summary: Spanish Prime Minister Sánchez faces corruption probes targeting colleagues and relatives, putting his eight-year premiership at risk.

headlines.headline: Russias Suspected Spy Church Near Swedish Airport Raises Alarm
headlines.summary: Swedish intelligence services warn that Moscow is likely using a Russian Orthodox church near Vasteras airport as a platform for espionage and hostile hybrid activities threatening Swedens national security.

headlines.headline: Macron Offers Truth Commission on Haitis Independence Ransom
headlines.summary: French President Emmanuel Macron announced a joint commission of French and Haitian historians to investigate the legacy of the crippling indemnity France extracted from Haiti for its independence, though he made no mention of Haitian demands for reparations.

headlines.headline: Russian Combat Losses in Ukraine Nearly Triple This Year
headlines.summary: Ukrainian officials report 145,000 Russian casualties in 2026, with losses of 179 per square kilometer of advance—far exceeding replacement capacity. Russia has also sold 27.9 tonnes of gold reserves to fund its war effort.

perspectives.angle: Nearly 500,000 Russian Soldiers Killed in Ukraine, Top UK Intel Chief Says
perspectives.summary: A senior British intelligence official provided a stark casualty figure, hardening the consensus that Russia is on its heels for the first time since the 2022 invasion.

headlines.headline: Spanish PM Sánchez Faces Political Crisis After Eight Years in Power
headlines.summary: Pedro Sánchez marks eight years as prime minister amid a wave of corruption investigations targeting allies and family members. The opposition has called for his resignation, though he insists he will complete his term.

headlines.headline: EU to release billions in frozen funds for Hungary amid reforms
headlines.summary: The EU agreed to release billions in frozen funds after Hungary's new government under PM Peter Magyar implemented reforms. Magyar called it a "historic" breakthrough.

headlines.headline: Zelensky's Decision to Honor WWII-Era Ukrainian Insurgent Army Sparks Outrage in Poland
headlines.summary: A presidential decree naming a Ukrainian military unit after the WWII-era Ukrainian Insurgent Army (UPA) has drawn sharp criticism from Poland, highlighting ongoing historical tensions between the two allies.

headlines.headline: EU Agrees on Tougher China Trade Policy as Beijing Vows Retaliation
headlines.summary: The European Union's six largest economies have agreed on stronger trade defense measures against China, with Brussels warning of a new "China shock" to European industries. Beijing has promised countermeasures.

headlines.headline: UN Blacklists Russian Forces Over Sexual Violence Against Ukrainian POWs
headlines.summary: A new UN report adds Russian forces to the sexual violence "blacklist" for crimes against Ukrainian prisoners of war and civilian detainees, documenting nearly 10,000 cases of conflict-related sexual violence worldwide.

perspectives.angle: UN blacklists Russian forces over sexual violence against Ukrainian POWs
perspectives.summary: The United Nations has added Russian forces to a blacklist over documented sexual violence against Ukrainian prisoners of war and civilian detainees, according to a new report.

headlines.headline: UAE Conducted Dozens of Airstrikes on Iran Using US and Israeli Intelligence
headlines.summary: The Wall Street Journal reports that the UAE carried out multiple strikes on Iranian targets, including the Asaluyeh petrochemical complex, using intelligence provided by the US and Israel during the Iran conflict.

headlines.headline: Lebanese Army Unable to Disarm Hezbollah, Israeli Army Sources Say
headlines.summary: Israeli military officials assess that the Lebanese Armed Forces lack the capability to disarm Hezbollah, raising concerns about the implementation of any ceasefire agreement in Lebanon.

headlines.headline: Hegseth assures Asian allies US seeks stable equilibrium against China hegemony
headlines.summary: US Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth told the Shangri-La Dialogue that Washington is determined to preserve an Indo-Pacific where "no state, including China, can impose hegemony."
